name: Paracetamol
usage: Used to treat many conditions such as headache, muscle aches, arthritis, backache, toothaches, colds, and fevers.
side_effects: Nausea, stomach pain, loss of appetite, itching, rash.
dosage: Adults: 500mg to 1g every 4-6 hours. Do not exceed 4g in 24 hours.

name: Ibuprofen
usage: Used to reduce fever and treat pain or inflammation caused by many conditions such as headache, toothache, back pain, arthritis, menstrual cramps, or minor injury.
side_effects: Stomach pain, heartburn, nausea, vomiting, gas, constipation, diarrhea.
dosage: Adults: 200-400mg every 4-6 hours. Do not exceed 1200mg in 24 hours unless directed by a doctor.

name: Amoxicillin
usage: A penicillin antibiotic that fights bacteria. It is used to treat many different types of infection caused by bacteria, such as tonsillitis, bronchitis, pneumonia, and infections of the ear, nose, throat, skin, or urinary tract.
side_effects: Nausea, vomiting, diarrhea, stomach pain, vaginal itching or discharge.
dosage: As prescribed by a doctor. Usually 250mg to 500mg every 8 hours.

name: Acetaminophen
side_effects: Liver damage (rare, especially with overdose), rash, nausea
dosage: Adults: 325-1000 mg every 4-6 hours as needed; not to exceed 4000 mg per day.

name: Aspirin
usage: Pain relief, fever reduction, anti-inflammatory, blood thinning
side_effects: Stomach upset, heartburn, bleeding, tinnitus (ringing in ears)
dosage: Adults: 325-650 mg every 4 hours as needed for pain/fever. For cardiovascular prevention: 81-325 mg once daily.

name: Lisinopril
side_effects: Cough, dizziness, fatigue, headache, kidney problems
dosage: Adults: 10 mg once daily, may increase to 20-40 mg daily.

name: Metformin
side_effects: Nausea, vomiting, diarrhea, stomach upset, vitamin B12 deficiency
dosage: Adults: Start with 500 mg once or twice daily with meals, may increase gradually.

name: Atorvastatin
side_effects: Muscle pain, diarrhea, nausea, headache, liver problems
dosage: Adults: 10-80 mg once daily.

name: Azithromycin
side_effects: Nausea, vomiting, diarrhea, stomach pain, dizziness
dosage: Adults: 500 mg on day 1, then 250 mg once daily for 4 more days (typical regimen).

name: Levothyroxine
side_effects: Heart palpitations, anxiety, weight loss, tremors, heat intolerance
dosage: Adults: Dosage varies widely based on individual needs, typically starting at 25-50 mcg once daily.

name: Omeprazole
side_effects: Headache, diarrhea, stomach pain, nausea, constipation
dosage: Adults: 20 mg once daily before a meal.

name: Simvastatin
side_effects: Muscle pain, liver problems, headache, nausea
dosage: Adults: 5-40 mg once daily.

name: Losartan
side_effects: Dizziness, fatigue, low blood pressure, cough (less common than ACE inhibitors)
dosage: Adults: 50 mg once daily.

name: Albuterol
side_effects: Tremors, nervousness, increased heart rate, headache
dosage: Adults: 2 puffs inhaled every 4-6 hours as needed.

name: Sertraline
side_effects: Nausea, diarrhea, insomnia, dizziness, sexual dysfunction
dosage: Adults: Start with 25-50 mg once daily, may increase up to 200 mg daily.

name: Escitalopram
side_effects: Nausea, insomnia, fatigue, dizziness, sexual dysfunction
dosage: Adults: Start with 5-10 mg once daily, may increase up to 20 mg daily.

name: Trazodone
side_effects: Drowsiness, dizziness, dry mouth, blurred vision, priapism (rare)
dosage: Adults: For depression: 150 mg/day in divided doses, may increase. For insomnia: 50-100 mg at bedtime.

name: Gabapentin
usage: Seizures, nerve pain (neuropathy), restless legs syndrome
side_effects: Drowsiness, dizziness, fatigue, weight gain, swelling
dosage: Adults: Dosage varies widely based on condition, typically starting at 300 mg three times daily.

name: Prednisone
side_effects: Weight gain, mood changes, increased appetite, thinning skin, increased risk of infection
dosage: Adults: Dosage and duration vary significantly. Typically starts higher and tapers down.

name: Warfarin
side_effects: Bleeding, bruising, rash, nausea
dosage: Adults: Dosage determined by INR monitoring, typically 2-10 mg once daily.

name: Loratadine
side_effects: Headache, drowsiness (less common than older antihistamines), dry mouth
dosage: Adults: 10 mg once daily.

name: Diphenhydramine
side_effects: Drowsiness, dizziness, dry mouth, blurred vision, confusion (especially in elderly)
dosage: Adults: 25-50 mg every 4-6 hours as needed for allergies. 25-50 mg at bedtime for insomnia.

name: Hydrochlorothiazide
side_effects: Dizziness, frequent urination, muscle cramps, low potassium
dosage: Adults: 12.5-50 mg once daily.

name: Furosemide
usage: Fluid retention (edema) due to heart failure, liver disease, or kidney disease
side_effects: Dizziness, frequent urination, muscle cramps, low potassium, dehydration
dosage: Adults: 20-80 mg once or twice daily.

name: Citalopram
side_effects: Nausea, insomnia, fatigue, dizziness, sexual dysfunction, QT prolongation
dosage: Adults: Start with 10-20 mg once daily, may increase up to 40 mg daily.

name: Fluoxetine
side_effects: Nausea, insomnia, headache, nervousness, sexual dysfunction
dosage: Adults: Start with 10-20 mg once daily, may increase.

name: Venlafaxine
side_effects: Nausea, insomnia, dry mouth, dizziness, increased blood pressure, sweating
dosage: Adults: Start with 37.5-75 mg once or twice daily, may increase.

name: Duloxetine
side_effects: Nausea, dry mouth, insomnia, dizziness, constipation, increased blood pressure
dosage: Adults: Start with 20-30 mg once or twice daily, may increase to 60 mg twice daily.

name: Tramadol
side_effects: Nausea, vomiting, constipation, dizziness, drowsiness, seizures (rare)
dosage: Adults: 50-100 mg every 4-6 hours as needed for pain; not to exceed 400 mg per day.

name: Oxycodone
side_effects: Constipation, nausea, vomiting, drowsiness, dizziness, respiratory depression
dosage: Adults: Dosage varies widely based on pain severity and formulation. Typically 5-20 mg every 4-6 hours.

name: Hydrocodone
side_effects: Constipation, nausea, vomiting, drowsiness, dizziness, respiratory depression
dosage: Adults: Dosage varies widely based on pain severity and formulation. Typically 5-10 mg every 4-6 hours.

name: Ciprofloxacin
usage: Bacterial infections (urinary tract, respiratory, skin, bone)
side_effects: Nausea, diarrhea, vomiting, headache, dizziness, tendon rupture (rare)
dosage: Adults: 250-500 mg every 12 hours or 500-750 mg every 24 hours, depending on infection.

name: Amoxicillin/Clavulanate
usage: Bacterial infections (ear, sinus, skin, urinary tract)
side_effects: Nausea, vomiting, diarrhea, rash, yeast infections
dosage: Adults: 500 mg/125 mg every 8 hours or 875 mg/125 mg every 12 hours.

name: Clarithromycin
side_effects: Nausea, diarrhea, abdominal pain, metallic taste, headache
dosage: Adults: 250-500 mg every 12 hours.

name: Mupirocin
usage: Topical antibiotic for skin infections (e.g., impetigo)
side_effects: Burning, itching, redness at application site
dosage: Adults: Apply a small amount to the affected area 2-3 times daily.

name: Lidocaine
side_effects: Skin irritation, numbness, burning at application site. Systemic: dizziness, drowsiness, confusion.
dosage: Topical: Varies by product and area. Injectable: Varies by procedure.

name: Hydrocortisone (topical)
side_effects: Skin thinning, stretch marks, burning, itching, dryness
dosage: Adults: Apply a thin layer to affected area 1-4 times daily.

name: Salicylic Acid
side_effects: Skin irritation, dryness, redness, peeling
dosage: Varies by product concentration and form (wash, cream, patch).

name: Benzocaine
usage: Topical anesthetic (sore throat, sunburn, minor cuts)
side_effects: Allergic reactions, methemoglobinemia (rare but serious)
dosage: Varies by product and application.

name: Dimenhydrinate
side_effects: Drowsiness, dizziness, dry mouth, blurred vision
dosage: Adults: 50-100 mg every 4-6 hours as needed.

name: Meclizine
side_effects: Drowsiness, dry mouth, fatigue
dosage: Adults: 25-100 mg once daily, usually taken 1 hour before travel.

name: Guaifenesin
usage: Expectorant (thins mucus, helps cough up phlegm)
side_effects: Nausea, vomiting, dizziness
dosage: Adults: 200-400 mg every 4 hours or 600-1200 mg extended-release every 12 hours.

name: Dextromethorphan
side_effects: Dizziness, drowsiness, nausea
dosage: Adults: 10-20 mg every 4 hours or 30 mg every 6-8 hours.

name: Pseudoephedrine
side_effects: Increased heart rate, nervousness, insomnia, high blood pressure
dosage: Adults: 30-60 mg every 4-6 hours or 120 mg extended-release every 12 hours.

name: Naproxen
usage: Pain relief, fever reduction, inflammation reduction
side_effects: Stomach upset, heartburn, dizziness, increased risk of bleeding
dosage: Adults: 220-550 mg every 8-12 hours as needed; not to exceed 1375 mg per day.

name: Celecoxib
side_effects: Stomach upset, diarrhea, headache, increased risk of heart attack/stroke
dosage: Adults: 100-200 mg once or twice daily.

name: Glipizide
side_effects: Low blood sugar (hypoglycemia), nausea, diarrhea, dizziness
dosage: Adults: Start with 5 mg once daily, may increase to 10-15 mg daily or 20 mg in divided doses.

name: Pioglitazone
side_effects: Weight gain, fluid retention, increased risk of heart failure, liver problems
dosage: Adults: 15-45 mg once daily.

name: Insulin (various types)
side_effects: Low blood sugar (hypoglycemia), weight gain, injection site reactions
dosage: Dosage is highly individualized and based on blood glucose levels, diet, and activity.

name: Glimepiride
side_effects: Low blood sugar (hypoglycemia), dizziness, nausea
dosage: Adults: Start with 1-2 mg once daily, may increase up to 8 mg once daily.

name: Sitagliptin
side_effects: Headache, runny nose, sore throat, stomach upset
dosage: Adults: 100 mg once daily.
